name: PowerPoint Deck
professionalExpectations: A PowerPoint deck must communicate a clear argument, audience-specific story, and polished visual hierarchy.
The first version should prioritize narrative, slide structure, proof, and reviewability before advanced animation.
Sources, claims, and owner-provided facts must be traceable when the deck is used for business or schoolwork.
Export, sharing, and branding decisions must remain under owner control.
minimumQualityBar: Purpose, audience, outcome, slide order, and key message are explicit.
Slides avoid crowded text, unsupported claims, inconsistent styling, and unclear calls to action.
Any citation or external source need is documented and must not be fabricated.
No private, customer, production, or restricted data is inserted without owner-approved handling.
commonModules: Audience brief, objective, outline, slide inventory, and speaker-note plan.
Title, problem, solution, proof, process, offer, roadmap, financial, and next-step sections when relevant.
Visual system with typography, colors, charts, iconography, and image direction.
Review checklist for accuracy, narrative flow, accessibility, and export readiness.
uxExpectations: Decks should be scannable from slide sorter view and readable in presentation mode.
Each slide should have one primary idea and an obvious visual focal point.
Charts and tables must be legible without relying on tiny labels.
Speaker notes should support delivery without hiding critical information.

components: PowerPoint or compatible deck generation only after outline approval
Canva or Base44 only as optional prototype tools when useful
Repository storage for source outline and review notes
No live sharing or publishing without owner approval
recommendedStack.notes: Start with outline and slide plan. Use external design tools only when approved and useful.

phases.name: Brief And Story
goals: Define audience, outcome, story arc, key messages, and evidence needs.
Identify any citation, confidentiality, or schoolwork constraints.

phases.name: Deck Draft
goals: Create slide outline, content hierarchy, and visual direction.
Review for factual accuracy, pacing, and design consistency.

phases.name: Export And Review
goals: Produce final deck artifacts only after owner approval.
Record source, review, and distribution constraints.

knownPitfalls: Making slides visually busy before the story works.
Fabricating sources, metrics, testimonials, or case details.
Using private or production data in a deck without classification.
Overbuilding animation or branding before the audience and outcome are clear.
approvalGates: Owner approval is required before using confidential, restricted, customer, or production data.
Owner approval is required before publishing, sharing externally, buying assets, or using paid design tools.
Owner approval is required before using branded client materials or private business facts.
Owner approval is required before claiming sources or citations that have not been verified.
qualityChecklist: Audience, objective, story, and next action are clear.
Slides are readable, consistent, and not overcrowded.
Claims, sources, numbers, and citations are verified or marked for owner review.
Export and sharing constraints are documented.
whatToBuildFirst: Narrative structure with one message per slide.
Low-risk visual system before final polish.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Animations, complex infographics, or detailed appendices before the story is approved.
Paid stock assets or design tools before approval.
Brand-heavy templates before content structure is clear.
stopConditions: Stop before using confidential, restricted, customer, production, or private source data without approval.
Stop before paid tools, external publishing, live collaboration sharing, or credential use.
Stop if citations are required but cannot be verified.
notes: Archetype knowledge record only. It does not publish, share, or buy presentation assets.
